title: Amauropelta nephelium

Amauropelta nephelium is generally found in moist, shaded areas and is often associated with tropical and subtropical regions.

intro: Amauropelta nephelium, also known as Nephrolepis nephelium, is a species of fern native to Australia and Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Amauropelta nephelium include various flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other secondary metabolites that contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Amauropelta nephelium is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are believed to promote respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis and asthma.
